currentState: Watchful escort professionalism. Cyndia still handles Jaina as a controlled risk, but the relationship has shifted from static detention to active field protection.
powerDynamic: Cyndia no longer owns the whole environment, but she still helps define how much freedom Jaina actually has: watched hands, saddle position, and survival warnings. Jaina's usefulness has risen, yet Cyndia remains part jailer, part bodyguard.

stateHistory.currentState: Guarded detention. Cyndia treats Jaina as a contained anomaly rather than a conventional prisoner, because the mage's behavior is too cooperative for sabotage and too disruptive to ignore.

physicalDynamics.pattern: Cyndia's side of the relationship is all evidence handling: staff confiscated, satchel secured, prisoner moved out of earshot of the road.
Jaina does not fight physically or attempt escape, forcing Cyndia to deal with an unusual kind of detainee — one more interested in continuing her work than in freedom.

Once the ride begins, Cyndia's physical relationship to Jaina becomes positional: she keeps the mage in formation, warns her to stay in the saddle, and covers the treeline while Jaina kneels to read the road.

nameUsage.cyndiaCallsJaina: The mage in chapter 1; 'Mage' as quick escort shorthand in chapter 2.
nameUsage.jainaCallsCyndia: Uses her name on-page once the ranger is part of the ride south.

history.event: Cyndia catches Jaina at the second ring with spellwork layered over the ward runes and detains her without reported violence.
Cyndia becomes the first person in chapter 1 to mediate Jaina's warning into a form Sylvanas can evaluate.
Cyndia physically participates in Jaina's release by dropping the ward-circle and returning her to the road under escort rather than to ordinary freedom.
During the wildlife attack and its aftermath, Cyndia helps keep Jaina alive long enough to continue diagnosing the contamination.

trajectory: Detention -> guarded field reliance. Cyndia still treats Jaina as a risk, but now that risk has to be moved, protected, and used.
